data.content:
[implementation] Take-loop dead-lease guard landed.

Files changed:
- src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts: added LeaseDeadDispatchFailure
  to the dispatch-failure union, with reasons lease_terminated,
  lease_state_unknown, lease_missing. Banner body renders all the
  metadata the user listed in the knot acceptance: sessionId,
  beatId, beatState, iteration, leaseId, leaseState, expectedStep,
  agentName, agentProvider, agentModel, agentVersion, followUpCount,
  promptSource, plus a per-reason remediation hint.
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-lifecycle.ts: added
  take_loop_follow_up_skipped_dead_lease lifecycle event name.
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-follow-up.ts: new evaluateLeaseHealth
  pure function (exported for testing) + LeaseHealthChecker interface
  (injectable). sendFollowUpPrompt is now async and runs the lease
  check before sending the follow-up. On non-healthy result it calls
  refuseFollowUpForDeadLease which emits the FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE
  banner of kind lease_dead, pushes it to the session UI buffer,
  records the new lifecycle event, and writes a
  lease_dead_on_followup audit entry. The healthy path is unchanged.
- src/lib/__tests__/take-loop-follow-up-dead-lease.test.ts: 10
  hermetic tests covering all evaluateLeaseHealth branches and the
  full banner contract for kind=lease_dead.
- src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-take-loop-follow-up.test.ts:
  small mock update — added vi.mock(@/lib/knots) returning a single
  lease_active lease, and set knotsLeaseId='test-lease-active' /
  knotsLeaseStep='implementation' in makeCtx so the existing
  foolery-6881 tests pass through the new dead-lease guard. The
  existing tests' original assertions are unchanged.

Behavior:
- Fail-closed: any of (no leaseId / lease not in list / lease state
  not in {lease_ready, lease_active}) => no follow-up sent + loud
  banner. The agent prompt embeds the lease id, so prompting with a
  dead lease guarantees the kno claim failure loop seen in
  maestro-ca91. Refusing is the only safe move.
- The healthy lease set is the literal set {lease_ready,
  lease_active} from observed kno output. Any future state additions
  by kno will fail-closed by default until added here, which is
  correct (better to over-refuse than over-send).

Quality gates:
- bunx tsc --noEmit -> clean
- bun run lint -> clean
- bun run test -> 3066 passed, 11 skipped, 42 todo (+10 new tests,
  no regressions; existing follow-up tests adapted via the mock
  update above).
- bun run build -> clean

Out of scope (per knot description):
- Does NOT diagnose the upstream cause (foolery-dd9c does that via
  snapshots).
- Does NOT auto-recover.
- Does NOT change kno semantics around rollback terminating leases.
